input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The local snapshot utility under `/app` spans a Go staging and restore service, a native directory-measurement helper, and shell drivers that compile the workspace before running audits. Restored directories are out of agreement with their sources across all six matrix cases (alpha, beta, gamma, delta, epsilon, and zeta). Get the pipeline back to a clean audit so running `bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh` writes `/app/output/restore-audit.json`.

The JSON has a top-level cases array for those six names. Each case reports name, source, restored, ok, digest, groups, and accounting. Source and restored are directory paths. Digest is an object with source and restored string fingerprints. Groups is an object with source and restored arrays of relative-path arrays; each inner array is sorted and names files sharing one hardlink backing, and each outer group list is ordered by the first path in its inner arrays.

Accounting is a flat array at the case level (not nested like groups): each entry has path, bytes, and period. Byte counts are per path, including hardlink names, rather than divided across a link group; each bytes value must match that path's on-disk file size in the corresponding source or restored directory. Period values use p1 for generated files under d/ when the case has moved into the later quota period, and p0 otherwise.

A case is clean only when ok is true and file contents, directory shape, hardlink groups, and accounting records all agree. The ok flag must reflect that full contract, not digest and hardlink agreement alone. Cases that replay staged rows across a restart, such as gamma, must end with the restored layout matching the source layout in every file's final content, not merely in directory shape.

      |                                                        ^
/app/probe/measure.c:56:9: note: ‘snprintf’ output 2 or more bytes (assuming 4097) into a destination of size 4096
   56 |         snprintf(child_full, sizeof(child_full), "%s/%s", root, child_rel);
      |         ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
+ args=("--out" "$out")
+ '[' -n '' ']'
+ '[' -n '' ']'

exit=0
root@07f74449b762:/app# sed -n '1,200p' /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
out="/app/output/restore-audit.json"
work=""
one_case=""
while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --out) out="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --work) work="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --case) one_case="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    *) shift ;;
  esac
done
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$out")"
make -s -C /app build
args=("--out" "$out")
if [ -n "$work" ]; then
  args+=("--work" "$work")
fi
if [ -n "$one_case" ]; then
  args+=("--case" "$one_case")
fi
/app/bin/snapctl "${args[@]}"
root@07f74449b762:/app# sed -n '1,200p' /app/Makefile
.PHONY: build audit clean

build:
        mkdir -p /app/bin
        gcc -std=c11 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -o /app/bin/fsmeasure /app/probe/measure.c /app/probe/fswalk.c
        go build -o /app/bin/snapctl ./cmd/ctl

audit:
        $(MAKE) -s build
        bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh

clean:
        rm -rf /app/output /app/bin /tmp/staged-snapshot-* /tmp/ssd-*
root@07f74449b762:/app#

#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
out="/app/output/restore-audit.json"
work=""
one_case=""
while [ "$#" -gt 0 ]; do
  case "$1" in
    --out) out="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --work) work="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    --case) one_case="$2"; shift 2 ;;
    *) shift ;;
  esac
done
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$out")"
make -s -C /app build
args=("--out" "$out")
if [ -n "$work" ]; then
  args+=("--work" "$work")
fi
if [ -n "$one_case" ]; then
  args+=("--case" "$one_case")
fi
/app/bin/snapctl "${args[@]}"
root@07f74449b762:/app# sed -n '1,200p' /app/Makefile
.PHONY: build audit clean

build:
        mkdir -p /app/bin
        gcc -std=c11 -O2 -Wall -Wextra -o /app/bin/fsmeasure /app/probe/measure.c /app/probe/fswalk.c
        go build -o /app/bin/snapctl ./cmd/ctl

audit:
        $(MAKE) -s build
        bash /app/scripts/run-matrix.sh

clean:
        rm -rf /app/output /app/bin /tmp/staged-snapshot-* /tmp/ssd-*
